bias: Financial conditions eased broadly today -- falling yields, calmer equity vol, a strong semis bid, and an oil pullback -- while a data fix (EXPINF2YR replacing an invalid T2YIE placeholder) shows the front-end real-rate picture was less restrictive than recently reported. Real 10Y yield stays elevated and the Korea gate remains shut 1/3.

supporting: 10Y yield -3.6bps to 4.541% with credit spreads flat-to-tighter
Enabling-layer chain up 5-12% across the board (DRAM, CRDO, ASX, GLW, LITE, AMKR, MU, APH, POWI)
TGA drew down ~$38.8B/wk, adding reserves back to the banking system
against: MOVE still climbing, +3.07% to 72.41 -- bond vol not confirming equity calm
USDJPY still above 162 BOJ intervention threshold
EXPINF2YR is model-based (Cleveland Fed), not a market breakeven -- treat as directional proxy, not exact substitute for a true 2Y breakeven
counterargs.read: Today's data points broadly toward easing conditions -- falling yields, calmer equity vol, a strong semis bid, and a pullback in oil. A data-quality fix also matters here: the front-end 'real-rate tightening pain trade' that's been the throughline of recent runs was partly an artifact of a stale placeholder breakeven. With EXPINF2YR now live, real 2Y yield reads 1.41%, well below the ~2.2%+ previously reported -- the front end looks less restrictive than believed. Bond-market vol (MOVE) is still climbing and the structural gates (Korea, BOJ threshold) haven't opened, so this isn't an all-clear, but both the price action and the corrected data argue for less alarm than recent runs implied.

trade_table.rationale: Broad 5-12% rally across DRAM, CRDO, ASX, GLW, LITE, AMKR, MU, APH, POWI; SOXX +4.9%, SOXX/SPY ratio rising to 0.786. Confirm follow-through before adding size.
10Y fell 3.6bps with breakevens stable -- consistent with an 'easing' read, not recession fear. MOVE still elevated keeps conviction capped.
HY OAS flat, HYG +0.19%; consumer-credit names up 2-5% today after a rough prior session.
Gate closed 1/3 -- EWY ~13pts below SMA20, KOSPI ~900pts below 8,200 trigger. No positioning until gate opens.
Still above 162 BOJ intervention threshold despite -0.20% pullback; headline risk remains live.

james_framework.what_market_IS_saying: Corrected data show real 2Y yield at 1.41%, not the ~2.2%+ implied by the prior stale placeholder -- meaningfully less restrictive at the front end than the multi-day 'pain trade' narrative suggested
james_framework.pain_trade: RETRACTED for now -- the 'real-rate tightening pain trade' thesis run over the past several sessions was built on a T2YIE placeholder (1.9516%) that isn't a real, trackable series (confirmed by user: not on FRED or TradingView). With EXPINF2YR live, the front-end real-yield picture is materially less tight than previously reported.
james_framework.watch_signal: Track EXPINF2YR day-over-day now that it's live on the watchlist to build a real trend baseline; avoid drawing directional conclusions until 3-5 sessions of clean data accumulate.

t2yie.label: 2Y expected inflation (Cleveland Fed EXPINF2YR)
t2yie.note: T2YIE is not a real FRED/TradingView series -- it doesn't exist. The user added EXPINF2YR (Cleveland Fed model-based 2Y expected inflation) to the watchlist as the working proxy. This replaces a stale 1.9516% placeholder that had been carried forward for multiple runs and was overstating real 2Y yields. Note: EXPINF2YR is model-based expected inflation, not a market breakeven, so it isn't perfectly apples-to-apples with T10YIE/T5YIE (which are market breakevens) -- but it's the best available live proxy.

real_yield_2y.trend_label: Corrected -- materially lower than prior stale estimate
real_yield_2y.note: Prior runs used a stale 1.9516% placeholder, overstating real 2Y yield at ~2.2%+ and feeding a 'real-rate tightening pain trade' narrative. With EXPINF2YR live, real 2Y yield is meaningfully lower at 1.41% -- undercutting that thesis. Treat this as a fresh baseline; no clean day-over-day trend yet.

breakevens.breakeven_curve_signal: Corrected 2Y read (EXPINF2YR) shows front-end real yield at 1.41%, well below the ~2.2%+ implied by the prior stale placeholder. Long-end breakevens flat-to-firm while nominal yields ease -- still consistent with a funding/liquidity-driven rally, but the front-end 'real-rate tightening' story looks overstated on corrected data.
